rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TIEN 1992 FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner's Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement is denied. Per the case law, there was no enforceable settlement agreement stated at the August 7, 2017 hearing for the purposes of CCP § 664.6, as the individual parties did not personally state their acceptance of all material terms of the settlement agreement as the terms were presented to the Court on August 7, 2017, or December 13, 2017. There is no single, signed written agreement under which to enforce an agreement under § 664.6, either. The Court has no authority under the cited case law or CCP § 664.6 to create a settlement agreement. The Court can determine issues of fact relevant to determining whether a settlement has been breached, but can only make a determination regarding enforcement under CCP §664.6 when there has been a meeting of the minds on each material settlement term. The parties are ordered to appear to select a date for an evidentiary hearing.
